channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris urges Black churchgoers in Georgia to head out to vote and gets an assist from Stevie Wonder
item.description: JONESBORO, Ga. (AP) — Kamala Harris on Sunday summoned Black churchgoers to turn out at the polls and got a big assist from music legend Stevie Wonder, who rallied congregants with a rendition of Bob Marley’s “Redemption Song.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Donald Trump said he's getting Kamala Harris for her birthday
item.description: Former president Donald Trump spent time serving food at McDonald's in Pennsylvania and wished Vice President Kamala Harris a Happy Birthday, joking he would get her flowers and fries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arnold Palmer's daughter reacts to Donald Trump's references to her father
item.description: One of the late golf legend Arnold Palmer's daughters calls Donald Trump’s references to her father’s genitalia "a poor choice of approaches” to honoring his memory.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris invokes Jimmy Carter in bid to get supporters to vote early
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris told supporters in Atlanta, Georgia, Saturday that former President Jimmy Carter had already cast his ballot in the election and they can too.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Ohio Gov. Bob Taft, scion of Republican family, backs Democrat Sherrod Brown for Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Former Ohio Gov. Bob Taft, scion of one of the state's best-known Republican families, threw his support Sunday behind Democratic Sen. Sherrod Brown in his hotly contested reelection race against GOP nominee Bernie Moreno.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In part of swing state Michigan, neighbors skip presidential signs in a heated campaign season
item.description: BLOOMFIELD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — The lawn of Nick Hannawa's suburban Detroit home is lined with political signs backing candidates for prosecutor, supervisor and local trustee. But Hannawa isn't promoting his presidential pick. He says he doesn't want the headaches in an polarized election year.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump works the fry station and holds a drive-thru news conference at a Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump has manned the fry station at a McDonald's in Pennsylvania before staging an impromptu news conference, answering questions from reporters through the drive-thru window.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump talks about Arnold Palmer's genitalia at Pennsylvania rally
item.description: Politicians saluting Palmer in his hometown is nothing new. Trump spent 12 full minutes doing so at the top of his speech in Latrobe on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, NewsNation

item.title: The US is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's attack plans
item.description: The United States is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents that assess Israel’s plans to attack Iran and note that Israel continues to move military assets in place to conduct a military strike in response to Iran’s blistering ballistic missile attack on Oct. 1.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Mike Balsalmo, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fetterman issues warning to Democrats after Elon stumps for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Pennsylvania Senator John Fetterman told the New York Post in an interview that Elon Musk's endorsement of former President Trump is "a concern" in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swing state official warns village struggling with financial losses after influx of illegal immigrants
item.description: The village of Lockland, Ohio says it is struggling with an influx of migrants from Mauritania, with what it says are dire financial consequences for the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Together they've been in office more than 70 years. Now their races could help swing House control
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One is the longest-serving woman in congressional history. The other is dean of the Republican delegation from California and the chairman of a subcommittee responsible for Pentagon spending. Together, they have more than seven decades of experience serving in Congress.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five takeaways from a fiery Wisconsin Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) and Republican challenger Eric Hovde squared off on a debate stage Friday evening in Madison in a fiery exchange of attacks that showed how intense this race has become after largely flying under the radar for months.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Lorenz: Musk draws more political ire than Cuban because of X
item.description: Musk has ardently endorsed Donald Trump, while Mark Cuban and Taylor Swift have thrown support behind Harris.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: A former Park Service ranger says U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump earned money from government sources through Washington hotel, Democrats find
item.description: Over 11 months while former President Trump was in office, his Washington, D.C., hotel took in some $300,000 from sources inside and outside of government, including tens of thousands in payments from the U.S. Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky Tiktok suit says company considered using KY creators to sway Sen. Mitch McConnell
item.description: The suit says videos by Kentucky small businesses were identified as possibly of interest to the senator, who has expressed concern about the app's effect on kids
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Goodman, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: US Senate candidates Brown, Rosen spar in Las Vegas debate
item.description: The showdown was the only face-to-face debate between Brown - the retired U.S. Army Captain - and Rosen in advance of the election on November 5.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SBA administrator visits Tampa Bay as disaster loan program runs dry
item.description: While visiting businesses in Tampa Bay, which included both of those businesses, SBA Administrator Isabel Casillas Guzman said the next move is up to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 25 Pinellas County voting locations change after hurricanes
item.description: Twenty-five polling locations in Pinellas County have been changed due to hurricanes Helene and Milton, according to the county supervisor of elections.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
